title: Types of Diabetes
focus: Classification of diabetes with special emphasis on the newly recognized Type 5 (Malnutrition-related/J-type) diabetes.
rationale: This section introduces a newly recognized classification of diabetes (Type 5) and contrasts it with traditional types, making it highly relevant for UPSC Science & Tech questions.
sectionExcerpt: Malnutrition-related diabetes has been officially recognized and named type 5 diabetes at International Diabetes Federation's (IDF) World Diabetes Congress. Also called J-type diabetes, is known to be associated with under nutrition and low weight. Other Types of Diabetes: Type 1: Autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells. Type 2: Mainly caused by obesity and over nutrition. Gestational: Occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy.
evidenceBullets: Type 5 diabetes is officially recognized by the IDF and is also known as J-type diabetes.
Type 5 diabetes is linked to chronic undernutrition and low BMI, especially during childhood or adolescence.
Type 1 diabetes involves autoimmune destruction of insulin-producing beta cells.
Type 2 diabetes is mainly caused by obesity and overnutrition.
Gestational diabetes occurs with high blood glucose during pregnancy.

title: Step-and-Shoot Spot-Scanning Proton Arc Therapy (SPArc)
focus: Application and benefits of SPArc in advanced radiation therapy for cancer treatment.
rationale: SPArc represents an advancement in cancer treatment technology (radiation therapy), a frequent topic in UPSC Science & Tech.
sectionExcerpt: For the first time, SPArc has been used to treat an Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma (ACC), a relatively rare form of cancer. About Step-and-Shoot Proton Arc (SPArc): A kind of advanced radiation therapy technique. It works by targeting tumours with protons while sparing surrounding tissue. Benefits: Improves the quality of radiation dose planning for various cancers like head and neck, brain, etc.
evidenceBullets: SPArc was used for the first time to treat Adenoid Cystic Carcinoma (ACC), a rare cancer.
SPArc is an advanced radiation therapy technique that damages cancer cells' DNA.
It targets tumors with protons while sparing surrounding healthy tissue.
It improves radiation dose planning for head, neck, and brain cancers.
